Test Description: Checks default reset values and write-read behavior for GPIO registers listed in addr_array using per-register read/write masks and skip lists; reports mismatches.
Remarks:
- SKIPPING VRRW registers (per comment in test_define.c)
- When reading default values the DIN value is becoming 1 automatically if we don't force any value; if we force zero to DIN, bit-level SEL becomes high, causing mismatch (per comment).
Test Steps / Procedure:
Runtime execution order:
- Entry: test_case()
  1) Call chk_rst_val():
     - For i = 0..CNT-1:
       - addr = addr_array[i]
       - If skip_rst_array[i] == 1: skip this address
       - If read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: skip (not readable)
       - data_rd = read_reg(addr)
       - data = (data_rd & 0xFFFFFFFE) // mask off bit0 before compare
       - If data == default_value_array[i]: pass; else { def_fail_cnt++ ; printf mismatch }
  2) Call chk_rd_wr():
     - For each write pattern j in [0xFFFFFFFF, 0xAAAAAAAA, 0x55555555, 0xF5F5F5F5, 0xA5A5A5A5, 0xFFFF0000]:
       2.1) Write phase:
            - For i = 0..CNT-1:
              - addr = addr_array[i]
              - If skip_array[i] == 1: skip
              - If write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: skip (not writable)
              - Else: write_reg(addr, (pattern & write_mask_array[i]))
       2.2) Read/verify phase:
            - For i = 0..CNT-1:
              - addr = addr_array[i]
              - If skip_array[i] == 1: skip
              - If write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: skip
              - If read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: skip
              - Else:
                - data_rd = (read_reg(addr) & read_mask_array[i])
                - wr_n   = (write_mask_array[i] ^ 0xFFFFFFFF)
                - exp_val = ((pattern & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i])
                             | (wr_n & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i]))
                - If data_rd == exp_val: pass; else { wr_fail_cnt++ ; printf mismatch }
  3) Completion:
     - If (def_fail_cnt > 0 || wr_fail_cnt > 0) finish(1) else finish(0)
- Delays: No wait_on() calls within chk_rst_val() or chk_rd_wr().
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- Default value check: For each address where readable and not skipped, masked read equals default_value_array[i] with bit0 cleared in comparison.
- Write/Read check: For each pattern and address where R/W enabled and not skipped, read-back value equals computed exp_val combining written bits (masked) and preserved default bits; wr_fail_cnt remains 0.
- Overall: finish(0) indicates pass; any mismatch increments counters and results in finish(1).
Gap Analysis: No negative tests; No error handling

Test Description: Verifies GPIO negedge interrupt per pin (GPIOs 8..39): configures input + negedge detect, generates a falling edge on each pin by toggling pad driver at 0xA0243FFC, waits with timeout, and validates in ISR that DIN is low, raw and group statuses set and cleared correctly.
Remarks:
- Bounded wait used instead of infinite loop; timeout comment suggests it may need adjustment to simulation time base.
- Pad driver register 0xA0243FFC is used to create edges.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Runtime execution order:
- Entry: test_case()
  1) Initialize:
     - test_err = 0
     - If GPIO0: GIC_EnableIRQ(87)
     - If GPIO1: GIC_EnableIRQ(88)
     - If GPIO0: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR)
     - If GPIO1: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR)
     - Drive all pads high: write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0xFFFFFFFF)
  2) Per-pin configuration loop (i = 0..31):
     - addr1 = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i * 4)
     - write_reg(addr1, (1<<20) | (1<<18) | (1<<16)) // doe=1, neie=1, iclr=1
     - wait_on(10)
  3) Test loop over pins (i = 0..31):
     - wr_val = (1u << i)
     - Pre-clear group raw: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, wr_val)
     - Enable only this pin: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, wr_val)
     - wait_on(10)
     - Arm wait: int_pend = 1
     - Create falling edge: write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0xFFFFFFFF); wait_on(30); write_reg(0xA0243FFC, ~wr_val)
     - Bounded wait: timeout = 5000; while (int_pend && timeout--) { wait_on(10) }
     - If (timeout == 0) { printf timeout error; test_err++ }
  4) Completion: finish(test_err)
- Interrupt service: Default_IRQHandler()
  A) local_wr = (1u << i) // compute mask
  B) int_pend = 0
  C) Return pads high: write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0xFFFFFFFF)
  D) raddr = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i * 4); rdata = read_reg(raddr)
  E) DIN check for negedge: if ((rdata & 0x1) != 0) { test_err++ }
  F) Raw bit check: if ((rdata & 0x2) != 0x0) {
       - rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1)
       - If ((rdata_grp & local_wr) == 0) { test_err++ }
       - Clear per-pin raw + keep doe: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4), (1<<20)|(1<<16))
       - Clear group raw: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, local_wr)
       - Verify group clear: rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); if (rdata_grp != 0x0) { test_err++ }
       - If GPIO0: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR); GIC_ClearIRQ(87)
       - If GPIO1: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR); GIC_ClearIRQ(88)
     } else { test_err++ }
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- No timeout occurs in the wait loop for any pin.
- In ISR: DIN bit low after falling edge; per-pin raw condition met (rdata & 0x2 != 0); group status bit set for current pin; after clears, group status reads 0x0; sysreg raw cleared; test_err remains 0; finish(0).

Test Description: Verifies GPIO posedge interrupt across all pads (GPIOs 8..39): enables posedge detection per pin, configures input mode via group IO_CTRL, enables group interrupts, then for each pin iteration creates a rising edge using 0xA0243FFC while ISR masks, clears, and validates group/raw and sysreg status with timeouts to avoid hangs.
Remarks:
- Group interrupt is masked during service to avoid re-entry; re-enabled after clearing.
- Bounded wait with timeout used per iteration; edges synthesized via 0xA0243FFC.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Runtime execution order:
- Entry: test_case()
  1) Enable IRQ lines:
     - If GPIO0: GIC_EnableIRQ(87)
     - If GPIO1: GIC_EnableIRQ(88)
  2) Enable sysreg interrupt output:
     - If GPIO0: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR)
     - If GPIO1: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR)
  3) Per-pin posedge enable (i = 0..31): write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4), 0x00020000) // peie=1
  4) wait_on(10)
  5) Configure input mode via group IO_CTRL: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1..4, 0x000000FF each)
  6) wait_on(10)
  7) Enable group interrupts: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0xFFFFFFFF)
  8) For i = 0..31 (test iterations):
     - Drive low: write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0x00000000); wait_on(10)
     - Arm: int_pend = 1
     - Create posedge: write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0xFFFFFFFF)
     - Bounded wait: timeout = 2000; while (int_pend == 1 && --timeout > 0) { wait_on(10) }
     - If timeout == 0: printf timeout; test_err++; break
     - Prep next: write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0x00000000); wait_on(10)
  9) Completion: finish(test_err)
- Interrupt service: Default_IRQHandler()
  A) wr_val = (1 << i); int_pend = 0
  B) Read group status: rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1)
  C) Mask group interrupt during service: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0x00000000)
  D) If (rdata_grp & 0xFFFFFFFF) != 0: success else { printf error; test_err++ }
  E) Clear per-pin raw for all: for j=0..31 write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (j*4), 0x00010000); wait_on(2)
  F) Verify group clear: rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); if (rdata_grp == 0x0) success else { printf error; test_err++ }
  G) Clear sysreg raw and verify:
     - If GPIO0: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR); rdata = read_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1); if (rdata & LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR) { printf error; test_err++ }
     - If GPIO1: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR); rdata = read_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1); if (rdata & LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR) { printf error; test_err++ }
  H) Re-enable group interrupts: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0xFFFFFFFF)
  I) Clear GIC IRQ: GIC_ClearIRQ(87/88 based on macro)
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- No timeout during wait loop; each iteration triggers group interrupt as expected.
- After per-pin raw clears, group status reads 0x0.
- Sysreg raw cleared for active GPIO instance; test_err remains 0; finish(0).
